rag_rules: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type scopes; use implementation scopes for assignments and output features.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use type-level guarantees for visible port dependencies.
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must reference only visible identifiers and declared helpers within the same annex scope.
Generated AGREE code should not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types not present in the model.
Fix root unresolved references before adding new clauses; remove invalid guarantees instead of adding architecture.
Use only valid AADL features or declared auxiliary variables in implementation-level assignments.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations; implementation annexes for bindings.
anti_patterns: Do not reference subcomponent paths or implementation-only features in component type annexes.
Do not add new architecture elements to fix AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about missing EOF
